findings.title: Diagnostic-clarity gap: race-with-Heavy warning lines do not annotate in-flight operation
findings.description: Round-1 finding #5 (lifecycleMutex serialization diagnostic clarity) was not explicitly addressed in the round-2 tech-spec. Decision 1 keeps the unknown-name skip silent and the non-Ready skip warning-logged with `status=Initializing|Failed|Idle`, but does not annotate the in-flight operation that caused the non-Ready state. Test `resetConversation_isSerializedByLifecycleMutex` (line 181) covers serialization behaviour, but does not assert the diagnostic message content distinguishes (a) a quiescent skip (engine never reached Ready) from (b) a race-with-Heavy skip (engine is mid cleanup+initialize). The Risks table row 3 acknowledges the race scenario but its mitigation only mentions waiting for first Ready signal — not improving the warning line. This is a diagnostic-quality issue, not an exploitable vulnerability; the closed whitelist + `sanitize()` + length bounds protect the channel itself. Severity is minor and identical to round 1 — flagging again because round-2 changes did not touch it. The orchestrator may dismiss this if diagnostic noise is acceptable for size-S phase; documenting it in patterns.md (per Task 5) is a low-cost alternative.
findings.location: Section: Decision 1; Risks table row 3; Task 1 description
findings.impact: Diagnostic clarity / debuggability. A future debugger triaging 'why did chat switch leak KV?' could be misled by a 'skipped reason=CHAT_SWITCH status=Initializing' line that was actually a transient race against an in-flight Heavy reinit, not a user-facing bug. Not a confidentiality / integrity / availability concern.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) extend Task 1 description: when constructing the non-Ready skip warning, append a best-effort `inflight=heavy-reinit|warmup|none` token derived from a lightweight registry-internal flag, or (b) document in patterns.md (Task 5 doc update) that 'skipped' reset lines next to a cleanup/initialize event in the same second indicate a benign race, not a leak. Option (b) is zero code cost and adequate for size-S. No security-critical fix required.
